results.q: A train leaves City A at 9:00 AM going 60 mph east. Another leaves City B at 9:30 AM going 75 mph west. The cities are 300 miles apart. When do they meet?
results.answer: The trains meet at11:13 AM.Here is the step-by-step breakdown:1.**Initial Gap:** The cities are300 miles apart.2.**Head Start:** The train from City A travels for0.5 hours (from9:00 AM to9:30 AM) before the second train starts.*Distance covered by Train A in0.5 hours: $60 \text{ mph} \times0.5 \text{ hours} =30 \text{ miles}$.3.**Remaining Distance:** The distance between the two trains at9:30 AM is $300 \text{ miles} -30 \text{ miles} =270 \text{ miles}$.4.**Relative Speed:** Since they are traveling towards each other, their speeds add up: $60 \text{ mph} +75 \text{ mph} =135 \text{ mph}$.5.**Time to Meet (After9:30 AM):***Time = Distance / Speed*Time = $270 \text{ miles} /135 \text{ mph} =2 \text{ hours}$.6.**Meeting Time:** They meet2 hours after9:30 AM, which is11:30 AM.****Correction: My initial calculation was slightly off. Let's re-verify the math using the derived value.**Initial Distance:300 miles*Train A speed:60 mph*Train B speed:75 mph*Train A starts0.5 hours early.Distance covered by A in0.5 hours:30 miles.Remaining distance at9:30 AM:270 miles.Combined speed:135 mph.Time to close270 miles: $270 /135 =2 \text{ hours}$.Meeting time:9:30 AM +2 hours = **11:30 AM**.

results.q: Write a Python function longest_run(nums) that returns the length of the longest run of consecutive equal numbers in a list. Include one example call with output.
